title: santolina chamaecyparissus subsp. pecten
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus subsp. pecten, also known as a type of cotton lavender or white lavender, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Santolina chamaecyparissus subsp. pecten include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. Santolina chamaecyparissus subsp. pecten can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
